page.text: Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited (BPCL) Mumbai has planned a revamp of the
existing CDU3 heater (F-101) to implement reliability enhancement recommendations at
A few mechanical modifications are envisaged in the fired heater for this capacity
enhancement, which are described elsewhere in the tender. Due to these mechanical
modifications, a few associated refractories also need to be relined. This specification
outlines the technical requirements for refractory relining for the contractor.
The contractor shall prepare refractory drawings, procure refractory materials & anchors,
carry out refractory application, and provide supervision, quality control services, and do
testing of the refractory samples. Contractor shall ensure mobilization of all resources in
Contractor’s scope of supply, manpower, tools
, and tackle at the site to complete the job
Heater-wise minimum refractory relining requirements in various areas are identified
below. This requirement has been firmed up based on the mechanical modification
envisaged. However, there could be other areas found out during the shutdown after
opening the heater and dismantling the existing refractory, where refractory relining will
be necessary. Refractory thickness and materials shall be as per the table. However, the
contractor shall verify the same with the existing, and if any difference shall be brought
to the notice of the owner for clarification, except for Type IV insulating castable, which
has been replaced by Type V.
As per the requirement 125 mm type I castable (cold
75 mm type I fire brick (hot face)
blanket type I A (1260 C grade,
As per the requirement As per the details in the approved
6-79-0007- EIL standard specification for castable application
6-79-0008- EIL standard specification for insulating castable
6-79-0003- EIL standard specification for fire bricks
6-79-0005- EIL standard specification for Ceramic fiber blanket
EIL standard specification for miscellaneous refractory and aux. material
6-79-0024- EIL standard specification for HSE requirements of refractory
6-79-0029- EIL standard specification for conventional dense castable
Contractor shall build complete scaffolding to provide an appropriate approach to all work
Contractor shall dismantle the refractory in the areas where refractory relining is
envisaged as per the list of work provided in the expected refractory work list.
shall be removed on all sides,
and the lining shall be relined.
Contractor shall prepare new module arrangement drawings showing stud spacing,
module orientation, etc., for the heater arch & wall, around new tube hanger locations
and tube inlet & outlet positions. Bolted-type modules may be considered at locations
There are locations in the convection section where new tubes could be inserted in the
future rows and/or a new provision for tubes needs to be created. In case of a new
provision for convection coils, the existing refractory lining of the wall is to be removed
500mm more from both ends of the new tubes.
In case new ITS needs to be installed in convection, it is required to remove the
surrounding refractory to a reasonable extent so that casting could be performed
In case the existing steam lancing is to be closed in convection, the refractory is to
be installed in that location after providing SS310 retainer plates all around.
It is envisaged that header boxes may also be constructed new or partially. Normally
header box castable is found to be very fragile after prolonged usage. Therefore, it will
be prudent to change the complete header box castable if it is not usable during
g) Floor refractory around burners and the new access door should be relined if damaged.
Contractor shall restrict the use of heavy machines for breaking, which may lead to
cracks/loosening of refractory in large areas.
Wherever the casing plate is opened up, the same shall be cleaned so that no deposits
Any perforation and/or thinning of casing plate more than the specified limit, if observed,
shall be brought to the knowledge of the owner for appropriate decision before
Inside casing plate, in contact with CF lining, shall be painted with epoxy phenolic paint
(one coat primer + two final coats) with a total DFT of 225 microns.
Contractor shall carry out a detailed inspection after entering the heater during shutdown.
Any refractory damage identified during shutdown in any location of the heater shall be
brought to the notice of the owner and shall be rectified in consultation with the owner.
Contractor shall also prepare an application procedure. Separate dry out for
floor/convection castable (for minimal application) is not required. However, initial
heating of the heater shall be slow (~15°C/hr) and 12-24 hours holding would be required
at 260° and 550°C. Detailed procedure to be prepared by the contractor.
Contractor shall procure all refractory/ancillary materials and do application at the site.
Refractory material shall be as per the respective EIL standard, and a compliance
datasheet shall be obtained from the manufacturer.
Refractory material shall be procured from EIL approved vendor list.
Refractory drawings and documentation to be reviewed before procurement and
p) In general, in the event of cut out to be made in the casing plate with refractory lining,
the existing lining shall be dismantled approximately 200-250 mm beyond the cutout line.
q) Wherever the existing refractory is dismantled due to any refractory modification, the
same shall be done up to a reasonable extent so that an appropriate refractory
r) Acid Resistance castable shall be replaced with 50% Al2O3 castable as per EIL standard
Castable should be installed by vibration in the shuttering. Shuttering should be strong
enough and suitably designed to withstand vibration during application.
In case of application by gunning, a procedure shall be established by a mockup in a
45-degree overhead panel, to be witnessed and approved by Client/EIL. Suitable
gunning grade material shall be used.
Clean drinking quality water shall be used for mixing of the castable. Water should be
cold enough so that the final mix temperature is maintained within 25
have a chloride content less than 200 ppm and a pH of around 6-8. Direct mixing of ice
with water shall be avoided. A suitable water chiller shall be used.
Castable lining (cement-bonded) shall be cured using a curing compound. Curing
compound shall be as per ASTM C 309, type II class B (resin-based). It shall be applied
on castable just after the lining is touch dry.
During the curing period of 24 hours and preferably for the next 24 hours of natural
drying too, there should not be any movement/ rotation of the lined equipment
The castable mix shall be within a temperature range of 15 °C to 25 °C. Material shall
be placed by vibration in the shuttering in panels. Panel joints shall be staggered.
During actual application, the temperature of the shell plate shall not exceed 32
Stoppage and termination of lining shall be as per the established procedure.
The schedule as specified for air drying, curing, and heat drying shall be ascertained.
Construction joints of the castable lining shall be staggered.
Prior to installation of castable lining, the surface shall be thoroughly cleaned. Proper
tools and tackles, such as a mixer, vibrator, etc., shall be used for installation.
Recommendation, if any, from the manufacturer shall be complied with and adhered
Prior to actual application, mock up shall be carried out for each type of castable
resembling the actual application conditions. Test samples shall be taken during each
mock-up, which shall be tested for Bulk density (BD) & Cold crushing strength (CCS)
after drying at 110°C/24 hours and Permanent linear change (PLC) at 800°C (or at
maximum specified temp, if value at 800°C is not specified in the approved data sheet
of material) as per IS 10570. Mock-up shall be witnessed by the EIL /Client refractory
Quality control shall be as per API 936 and 6-79-0007.
The test results of the mock-up samples shall meet the specification. Parameters
maintained in the successful mock-up, i.e., water percentage, mixing time, application
methods, etc., shall be maintained during actual application.
There shall be no gap within the lining.
Individual cut bricks shall not be less than half of the original size.
If required, bricks may be cut to suit at the site. A proper brick-cutting machine with
diamond wheels shall be used for the purpose.
After all activities, the top layer bricks of the existing floor lining shall be removed,
Anchor spacing to be verified as per the approved drawing
Anchor welding procedure and type of welding electrodes shall be verified
Locking arrangement for the blanket shall be checked for tightness.
Back-up blanket joints shall have at least 13mm compressed butt joints.
At no location, a blanket from the edges shall be left in hanging position. The distance
between the stud and blanket edges shall strictly be maintained
Folds shall always be kept towards the hot face side, and the same shall be verified.
Module systems shall be installed so that joints at each edge (along the folding direction)
are compressed by 5 mm to avoid gaps due to shrinkage.
Hand packing of gaps /voids after module installation is not permitted. If necessary,
modules shall be removed and reinstalled.
Compression bands of the CF module shall not be removed until the completion of arch
lining. After removal of the compression bands, tamping shall be done on the hot face of
the CF module to eliminate the gaps.
During actual application of castable, test samples shall be taken for each day’s
installation and kept in an orderly manner with proper traceability. One set of test
samples shall consist of three samples for BD & CCS. The sample shall be selected at
random and tested for BD & CCS (after drying at 110°C/24 hours).
Application of castable lining shall meet the requirements of API RP 936. Testing of
castable samples shall be as per the test standards mentioned in the approved
datasheet. All testing charges shall be borne by the contractor.
Tolerances of insulating castable properties for production specimen:
Castable-lined items shall be hammer tested for soundness. Tolerance on the width and
depth of cracks in castable linings: max. 1.5 mm wide and up to half the lining thickness
deep. Beyond this limit, it shall be repaired as per standard procedure.
Lining thickness and other dimensions of lining shall be checked, and the same shall be
Physical verification of bricks with reference to shape, size, cracks, broken corners,
surface imperfections, etc., shall be carried out.
The following are the tolerances for the acceptability of fabrication and erection of the
Tolerances on expansion gap O/S burner tiles: +2.0 mm, -0.0 mm
Tolerances on expansion gaps for bricks: +3.0, -0.0 mm
Quality of welding strength of anchors shall be tested by randomly striking with a hammer
and bending from the mean position.
CF lining shall be inspected for integrity, pattern of modules, thickness, etc.
EIL refractory specialist may inspect the ongoing/final application as per the
instruction. However, the contractor shall depute an experienced refractory
supervisor/inspector during refractory installation and shall report any abnormalities /
submit daily quality log sheets to the owner on a day-to-day basis. Any defects identified
appointed inspector at any stage of application shall be
rectified by the contractor without any time and cost implications.
Contractor shall prepare HSE risk assessment analysis and requirements, which shall
be part of the application procedure. HSE requirements shall be considered for refractory
storage, mixing, application, dry out, anchor welding, etc.
Contractor shall follow EIL standard 6-79-0024 for HSE requirements for all the activities
Contractor shall submit the following to EIL for review:
Refractory product datasheet and compliance datasheet.
Refractory modification drawings are to be submitted.
Application & dry out procedure, inspection test plan for application
